macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: SpaceX Leads Global Tech Selloff as Market Value Erases $600B
items.summary: SpaceX shares fell for a third consecutive day, erasing $600 billion in market value and triggering a broader tech selloff; the MSCI Index dropped 2.3% and Nasdaq futures declined 1.3% amid hawkish Fed signals.

items.title: Fed Hawkish Message Drives Market Volatility as Yen Hits 1986 Low
items.summary: Markets reacted to the Federal Reserve's hawkish stance, pushing the Japanese Yen to its lowest level since 1986 and causing Treasury yields to gain after a recent holiday dip.

items.title: US Grants Iran 60-Day Oil Export Waiver Amid Peace Talks
items.summary: The U.S. Treasury issued a waiver allowing Iran to export oil until August 21, coinciding with progress toward a lasting peace agreement between Washington and Tehran; Brent crude held below $78/barrel.

items.title: KOSPI Plunges Over 8% as Global Equities Melt Down
items.summary: The South Korean KOSPI index fell more than 8% in morning trading, reflecting a broader global equity melt-down as commodities and high-momentum stocks like SpaceX continue to decline.

items.title: Earnings Remain Key Market Support Despite Tech Selloff
items.summary: Charles Schwab analyst Liz Ann Sonders emphasized that corporate earnings continue to provide the most critical underlying support for markets, even as tech stocks face significant pressure.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Dips Below $63K Amid Record ETF Outflows and Market Volatility
items.summary: Bitcoin crashed below $63,000, sparking fears of a drop to $60,000, while spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded their largest monthly redemption wave since launch with $6.35 billion in 30-day outflows. The market saw $107 million in liquidations, including a whale shorting $48.78 million in BTC, SOL, and ETH with 20x leverage.

items.title: Bank of England Drops Individual Stablecoin Holdings Limits in Favor of Systemic Ceiling
items.summary: The Bank of England abandoned its controversial proposal to restrict individual stablecoin holdings, replacing it with a single massive £40 billion ceiling on systemic coin issuance to reduce regulatory friction.

items.title: Coinbase CEO Calls for Accredited Investor Changes as Truth Social ETF Relaunches
items.summary: Coinbase's CEO is advocating for changes to accredited investor rules, while the Truth Social God Bless America ETF (ARCA:YALL) officially relaunches as part of Trump Media & Technology Group's strategic fintech expansion.

items.title: Franklin Templeton Launches Franklin Crypto After Acquiring CoinFund Spinoff 250 Digital
items.summary: Franklin Templeton completed its acquisition of 250 Digital, a CoinFund spinoff, and formally launched Franklin Crypto, an active digital-asset management division built specifically for institutional investors.

items.title: THORChain Resumes Trading After $10.7M Exploit with Monero Integration Planned
items.summary: THORChain has resumed trading more than a month after a $10.7 million exploit, with native Monero integration currently in development to enhance privacy features.

items.title: Pepeto Secures Dual Exchange Listing Ahead of Binance Debut
items.summary: Emerging crypto presale project Pepeto has secured a second major cryptocurrency exchange listing to run alongside its highly anticipated debut on Binance, signaling strong market interest.

items.title: Major Altcoins Decline: Ethereum Down 6%, Solana and XRP Drop Over 2%
items.summary: Ethereum plunged 6% daily, while Solana fell 2.3% and XRP dropped 1.1% over the past 24 hours, contributing to a total crypto market cap decline of 2.7% to $2.2 trillion.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Robots Now Gather Data at Human-Unmatchable Scale; AI Agents Accelerate Scientific Discovery
items.summary: A breakthrough in machine learning reveals that robots can collect data at scales impossible for humans, with AI agents helping researchers decide next steps—potentially compressing timelines for drug development and biotech leadership.

items.title: AI Race Spreads Across G7, Politics, and Infrastructure; OpenAI and Anthropic Expand Globally
items.summary: AI CEOs appeared at the G7 summit while OpenAI expanded its partner network and Anthropic deepened its presence in Korea, signaling that AI capability is outpacing policy and control frameworks.

items.title: Oracle Cuts 21,000 Jobs Amid AI Transformation; New Select AI Enhancements Require 26ai Release
items.summary: Tech giant Oracle is eliminating 21,000 jobs as it embraces AI, while its Select AI NL2SQL capabilities now depend on vector-based semantic similarity search available only in the upcoming 26ai release.

items.title: Defense Intelligence Agency (DIA) Launches RFI for AI-Powered Procurement Platform
items.summary: The DIA issued a request for information to develop an AI prototype project aimed at streamlining procurement and eliminating drudge work, with industry white papers due July 2.

items.title: MIT Researcher Emanuele Sansone Addresses 3D World Understanding as Key ML Challenge
items.summary: Building AI models that understand and interact with the 3D world remains a major open challenge, requiring progress in learning from small data, efficient inference over discrete structures, and generating creative hypotheses.

items.title: US AI Executive Order, China's $295B AI Buildout, and India's Sarvam AI Unicorn Highlight Global Trends
items.summary: Daily AI news highlights include a new US AI Executive Order, China's massive $295 billion AI infrastructure investment, and India's Sarvam AI achieving unicorn status amid shifting global education and tech policies.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Sony Announces Nioh 3, Bloodstained: The Scarlet Engagement, and Final Fantasy Tactics Remaster at State of Play
items.summary: During Sony's June State of Play, Team Ninja revealed Nioh 3 (coming 2026 with a demo now), ArtPlay announced Bloodstained: The Scarlet Engagement (2027), and Square Enix confirmed Final Fantasy Tactics: The Ivalice Chronicles remaster for September 2026.

items.title: Arc System Works Unveils New Marvel Tag Fighter with 4-Person Teams
items.summary: Arc System Works and Sony Interactive Entertainment announced a brand-new fighting game featuring Marvel characters and tag battles, allowing teams of four players, though fans of Marvel vs. Capcom expressed disappointment over the lack of a direct MVC sequel.

items.title: Xbox Studios Ninja Theory, Double Fine, and Compulsion Games Reportedly Closing
items.summary: Xbox is reportedly shutting down first-party studios Ninja Theory, Double Fine, and Compulsion Games, marking a significant shift in Microsoft's studio portfolio and raising concerns about the future of indie-focused Xbox development.

items.title: Valve's Steam Machine Launches at $1,049, Significantly Over Budget
items.summary: Valve's new Steam Machine starts at $1,049 for a 512GB model without a controller, with a 2TB version including a controller costing over $1,400, far exceeding the company's original pricing goals.

items.title: Silent Hill f Release Date Revealed, Arriving Soon
items.summary: The release date for Silent Hill f has been officially revealed, with the game arriving much sooner than fans anticipated, adding to the year's lineup of horror titles.

items.title: Ubisoft Co-Founder Claude Guillemot Dies in Plane Accident
items.summary: Claude Guillemot, one of the five founding Guillemot brothers and a co-founder of Ubisoft, has passed away following a plane accident, marking a somber moment for the gaming industry.

items.title: Sega Shifts Strategy to Influencers After Sonic and Shinobi Sales Disappoint
items.summary: Sega is pivoting its marketing strategy toward influencers after the sales of Sonic and Shinobi games fell short despite strong reviews, while confirming two unannounced flagship IP games are coming soon.

items.title: EA Creates New Division to Push In-Game Advertising
items.summary: Electronic Arts has established an entire new division dedicated to increasing in-game advertising, signaling a major industry shift toward monetization through ads in live-service and traditional games.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: FAA Announces Major Software Upgrade for Nationwide Air Traffic Control Systems
items.summary: The FAA confirmed today it will begin rolling out a major software upgrade to air traffic control systems nationwide in the fall, with full implementation expected by the end of 2028.

items.title: Tesla Crashes Into Texas Home, Killing Woman; Driver Manually Overrode Self-Driving Feature
items.summary: A Tesla vehicle crashed into a home in Texas, killing a woman inside; video evidence shows the driver manually overrode the self-driving feature by pressing the brake.

items.title: SpaceX Shares Slip for Third Day Amid Global Tech Selloff
items.summary: SpaceX shares declined for the third consecutive day, shedding hundreds of billions in value as tech stocks lead a broader market selloff, with SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics among the biggest losers.

items.title: New Traffic Management System 'Traffic Eye' Launched in Nepal
items.summary: A new traffic management system called 'Traffic Eye,' developed by Shubham and his team at Lumbini, has been launched in Nepal, emphasizing that following traffic rules is free while breaking them is expensive.
